contracts: record_commit(task_id, commit_hash, repo_path="") -> {ok, task_id, commit_hash} or {error}
handle_get_commits(task_id) -> list of {commit_hash, repo_path, logged_at}, most recent first
commit-task-traceability-capture.description: commit_task_map table (task_id, commit_hash, repo_path, logged_at; UNIQUE(task_id, commit_hash)) records which commit SHA implemented which task, populated post-commit by hooks/dispatcher.py (_record_bash_commit for Bash git-commit calls, _record_mcp_commit for the MCP git__commit tool from any server), queryable via the tasks__get_commits MCP tool (handle_get_commits). Complements GitCommitGate/gates-commit-traceability, which only enforces task:<id> presence pre-commit — this concept is the post-commit half that closes the loop.
invariants: INSERT OR IGNORE via the UNIQUE(task_id, commit_hash) constraint makes recording idempotent — safe to call twice for the same commit

record_commit() returns an error dict (never raises) if task_id is unknown or either field is empty; both dispatcher capture functions treat any failure as fail-open (log a warning, never block the tool response)
Table name deliberately avoids task_commits — that name was already used (and removed) for an unrelated LangGraph checkpoint-state key (task:c5628e95)
commit-task-traceability-capture.name: commit-task-traceability-capture

contracts: config.memory_db, config.tasks_db, config.tool_hints_db, config.log_db are valid Paths
config.cwd_domain_map returns the mtime-cached dict loaded from cwd_domains.json (icloud_db_dir), falling back to _CWD_DOMAIN_MAP_DEFAULT if missing/unreadable
config-db-paths-and-domains.description: Central configuration via pydantic-settings. Defines all database paths, valid domains, CWD-to-domain mapping, and memory scoring tunables. All other modules import from config singleton.
invariants: cwd_domains.json (icloud_db_dir, same pattern as memory_scoring.json) is the source of truth for repo-to-domain classification — add an entry there when onboarding a repo, no code change/redeploy needed
_CWD_DOMAIN_MAP_DEFAULT is only a fallback seed, used if cwd_domains.json is missing or fails to parse
Config is frozen (immutable after construction)
config-db-paths-and-domains.name: config-db-paths-and-domains

contracts: migrate_memory_db(conn), migrate_tasks_db(conn), migrate_tool_hints_db(conn) each take an open sqlite3.Connection
db-schema-ddl-and-migrations.description: Central DDL constants and additive migration functions for all SQLite databases (MEMORY.sqlite, proj_tasks.db, tool_hints.sqlite, claude_hooks.sqlite). Tests import DDL from here — no inline DDL in test files.
invariants: All migrations are additive-only (ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN) — never destructive
migrate_*() functions are safe to run repeatedly (idempotent)
DDL constants are the authoritative schema definition
db-schema-ddl-and-migrations.name: db-schema-ddl-and-migrations

contracts: Reads hook payload from stdin, writes JSON to stdout
Returns additionalSystemPrompt for UserPromptSubmit
Returns permissionDecision=deny for blocked PreToolUse
PreToolUse handlers may also return permissionDecision=allow with additionalContext to inject a non-blocking reminder into Claude's context without denying the tool call
dispatcher-hook-event-routing.description: Single entry point for all Claude Code hook events. Routes each event type (UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, SessionEnd) to its dedicated handler via a dispatch table.
invariants: Every hook event must map to exactly one handler in _HANDLERS
PreToolUse handlers that fail on irreversible tools must deny, not allow
Handlers return None to pass through or a dict with hookSpecificOutput to act

Two distinct PreToolUse response categories: gates (deny-capable checks like _check_task_body_format, run_gate) must resolve to a strict allow/deny decision; reminders (like _maybe_cache_reminder for Write/Edit/MultiEdit, and _maybe_agent_cache_reminder for Task and Agent — nudging to check prompt_cache__lookup/search before spawning a subagent) are allow-only and use additionalContext to surface non-blocking context — reminders never deny

Two distinct handler return shapes exist: PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit handlers return None or a dict wrapping hookSpecificOutput; Stop handlers (_handle_stop) return None or a plain top-level {"decision": "block", "reason": ...} dict per Claude Code's Stop-hook schema (not wrapped in hookSpecificOutput). The turn-completion chime no longer uses Stop-hook blocking: since 150df2a the sound plays server-side via PlaySoundNode (detached afplay subprocess, start_new_session=True — no Claude round-trip). NoopNode marks the first Stop of a turn by setting state['stop_alert_sent']=True, which gates PlaySoundNode (next node in the stop chain) so the chime fires exactly once per turn; sound_played guards replays within the same turn, and run_session() resets stop_alert_sent=False on the next UserPromptSubmit. run_stop() in session_graph.py still surfaces-and-clears pending_hook_output for any node that sets one (task:aacf3ef3 history; refactored by 150df2a).

PostToolUse also runs unconditional side-effect capture functions before the mcp__-prefix gate: _record_bash_commit (Bash tool, matches _GIT_COMMIT_RE/_TASK_ID_RE, shells out to git rev-parse HEAD) and _record_mcp_commit (short_name == "git__commit" from any MCP server, reads commit_sha directly from tool_response). Both call src.tools.tasks.record_commit() to insert into commit_task_map — a direct dispatcher-level function call, not a LangGraph node, and not routed through the __hook__/mcp_hook_bridge convention (that convention is one-way, prompt-injection only, no side effects). task:a2fd402d
dispatcher-hook-event-routing.name: dispatcher-hook-event-routing

contracts: Returns a string (may be empty) to be injected as additionalSystemPrompt
All sections are markdown-formatted
dispatcher-system-prompt-assembly.description: Assembles the injected additionalSystemPrompt from SessionState context: Turn state, cached-answer availability note (from CacheCheckNode's cache_hit), soul/vault context, active domains, injected memories, tool hints, active task body, task history, related commits, and active review checklist. Also emits a one-time "New project detected" note (from CwdDomainDetectNode's cwd_unmapped) when the session's cwd matches no cwd_domains.json entry. Also renders execution_contract (added 2026-07-05, task:380140fa) as its own "### Execution contract" section, verbatim, distinct from task_body.
evidence: hooks/dispatcher.py: def _enforce_context_budget(ctx: dict) -> None: ... while memories and _combined_tokens() > _CONTEXT_TOKEN_BUDGET: memories.pop()
hooks/dispatcher.py: contract = (ctx.get("execution_contract") or "").strip(); if contract: lines.append("### Execution contract")
invariants: Sections are only emitted when their data is non-empty

Related tasks section is populated when LoadRelatedTasksNode finds semantically similar done tasks via TurboVec neighbor search (capped at 3); empty when no active task, no rag index, or no matching done neighbors
Task context summary takes precedence over raw task history when both exist

Cached answer section is emitted when ctx['cache_hit'] is non-empty (set by CacheCheckNode in langchain_learning/nodes/cache_check.py); distinguishes match_type exact/fuzzy and picks commits_behind (source='code') vs age_days (source='websearch') as the staleness signal

New-project note is emitted when ctx['cwd_unmapped'] is true (set by CwdDomainDetectNode only on the first turn a session's cwd is unmapped, tracked via cwd_domain_reminder_sent in checkpoint state so it fires once per session, not every turn)

Before assembly, _enforce_context_budget(ctx) trims ctx['memories'] from the tail (lowest-scored, since the list is pre-sorted descending) until the combined token count of memories+task_memories+related_tasks+related_commits+task_rag_chunks fits _CONTEXT_TOKEN_BUDGET (4000 tokens), or memories is empty. related_tasks/related_commits/task_rag_chunks are never trimmed here — they're already capped at top-3 by their nodes.

task_body is hard-truncated to _TASK_BODY_CHAR_CAP (3000 chars) at render time, appending an "...[truncated]" marker when cut — it has no upstream length cap (a task's body can be arbitrarily long, e.g. a large epic scaffold).

mid_task_decisions (Task decisions section) is capped to the most recent _MAX_DECISIONS (15) entries by langchain_learning/nodes/decision_task.py, sliced on every tasks__add_decision append — not trimmed in dispatcher.py itself. This checkpoint state exists solely for prompt injection; the durable decision history lives in the task's DB record, unaffected by the slice.

execution_contract is rendered as its own section, independent of task_body and _enforce_context_budget — it is NOT subject to _TASK_BODY_CHAR_CAP truncation or memories-budget eviction, by design (a fixed north-star template built once at task activation by activate_task.py._build_execution_contract, must stay byte-identical every turn). Cleared alongside the other active-task fields at all three deactivation sites: DeactivateTaskNode (tasks__clear_active/tasks__finish), ActivateTaskNode's pop-to-empty-stack, and LogTaskEventsNode's "task:<id> done" auto-close (task:eb46ac47 — this third site was originally missed).

After _format_system_prompt, the UPS handler appends one-shot node output: if ctx['pending_hook_output'].hookSpecificOutput has hookEventName == 'UserPromptSubmit', its additionalContext is appended to the assembled system prompt (e.g. LogTaskEventsNode's /task-introspection nudge after a "task:<id> done" auto-close, task:eb46ac47). run_session() surfaces-and-clears the checkpoint copy (mirroring run_post_tool/run_stop), so the payload renders exactly once and cannot leak into the next PostToolUse response; the hookEventName gate prevents a stale cross-event payload from ever being rendered here.
dispatcher-system-prompt-assembly.name: dispatcher-system-prompt-assembly

contracts: Returns None to allow or a deny hookSpecificOutput dict
Deny message includes the full template scaffold for the detected type
dispatcher-task-body-gate.description: Pre-tool gate that enforces task body format for tasks__create. Parses task_templates/*.md at import time to derive required section labels per task type. Denies creation if body is missing Type: line or required sections.
invariants: task_templates/*.md is the single source of truth for required sections
Gate only applies to claude-hooks domain tasks (other domains pass through)
Gate runs before JiraHierarchyGate in the PreToolUse chain
dispatcher-task-body-gate.name: dispatcher-task-body-gate

contracts: Returns (True, reason) for commits missing task:<id>
Returns (False, '') for all non-commit Bash calls
gates-commit-traceability.description: GitCommitGate enforces that every version-control commit (via Bash or the MCP tool) includes a task:<id> reference for traceability. Regex detects commit commands; passes through all non-commit Bash calls immediately.
invariants: Every commit must reference task:<id> in the command string
Non-commit Bash calls are never blocked by this gate
gates-commit-traceability.name: gates-commit-traceability

contracts: validate_jira_hierarchy(issue_type, parent_id) returns None (valid) or error string
gates-jira-hierarchy.description: Enforces Jira-style parent-child issue type rules on tasks__create. Stories/tasks/bugs must have epic parents; subtasks must have story/task/bug parents; epics cannot have parents. validate_jira_hierarchy() is the single source of truth shared with the UI.
invariants: Epic has no parent
story/task/bug parent must be epic
subtask parent must be story, task, or bug
feedback can attach to any type
gates-jira-hierarchy.name: gates-jira-hierarchy

contracts: check(tool, ctx) returns (deny: bool, reason: str)
deny=True means the tool must be blocked; reason is shown to Claude
gates-prereq-chain-enforcement.description: Anti-hallucination send-gate system. Verifies tool calls against session history before allowing them. Each gate is a composable chain of verifier factories loaded from gate_rules.yaml or hardcoded subclasses.
invariants: Gates operate only on tool call records written by the hook infrastructure, never on model memory
External gate rules from gate_rules.yaml never override internal Python gates
All gates fail open on DB errors — a broken gate never blocks tools
gates-prereq-chain-enforcement.name: gates-prereq-chain-enforcement

contracts: Callers reading hook_logs (e.g. hooks__read_logs_sqlite MCP tool) should treat it as a rolling ~40-50k row window of recent activity, not a complete historical log -- older entries are permanently gone once pruned, not archived.
hook-logs-sqlite-autoprune-at-50k.description: SQLiteHandler.emit() writes every log record to hook_logs in claude_hooks.sqlite (iCloud-synced), then checks COUNT(*) on every single insert. Once the table reaches 50,000 rows, it prunes down to the newest 40,000 rows in one DELETE, keyed by ts DESC (not id DESC).
invariants: hook_logs is capped at 50,000 rows -- it auto-prunes to 40,000 on the insert that crosses the threshold, so the table oscillates between ~40k and 50k rows over time, never growing unbounded.
Pruning keeps the newest rows by ts (timestamp), not by id/rowid -- id values become non-contiguous after a prune, so id (or max(id)) is not a valid proxy for total row count or table age.

The COUNT(*) check runs on every emit() call (every log line), not on a schedule/batch -- this is an O(n) check per write, acceptable at this volume but a scaling consideration if log volume grows sharply.
Pruning is silent -- emit() swallows all exceptions (bare except: pass), so a failed prune or count-check never surfaces as an error.
hook-logs-sqlite-autoprune-at-50k.name: hook-logs-sqlite-autoprune-at-50k

contracts: Usage: python3 client.py <HookEvent>
Reads hook payload JSON from stdin, POSTs to {CLAUDE_HOOKS_SERVER or http://127.0.0.1:8766}/hook/{EVENT}, prints the response JSON to stdout
hooks-client-cwd-forwarding.description: Thin HTTP client for the FastAPI hook server. Reads the hook JSON payload from stdin (which already carries the real per-session cwd from Claude Code) and POSTs it to localhost:8766/hook/{event}.
invariants: CLAUDE_CWD env var is a fallback only, used when payload['cwd'] from stdin is missing/empty — it must never unconditionally overwrite a cwd Claude Code already supplied, since CLAUDE_CWD reflects whatever cwd the long-running server process happened to launch from, not the calling session's cwd (bug found 2026-07-08: unconditional overwrite clobbered every session's reported cwd with a stale/empty env var)
Fails open: if the server is unreachable, exits 0 with an empty JSON object rather than blocking the hook event
hooks-client-cwd-forwarding.name: hooks-client-cwd-forwarding

contracts: handle_server_memory(n_events) returns a markdown table or {error: ...}
handle_read_logs_sqlite(level, logger, search, limit) returns {count, rows}
handle_session_id() returns {session_id, turn} or {error: ...} after one retry on empty first response
hooks-mcp-tools-observability.description: MCP tools for querying hook state: server_memory (live chronological timeline via HTTP), checkpoint_query (deprecated SQLite checkpoint reader), and read_logs_sqlite (filtered log reader from claude_hooks.sqlite). Also session_id (added 2026-07-05, task:61703ef7): returns current session_id via GET /session/current with a built-in one-shot retry, absorbing the checkpoint-write race for a brand-new session.
invariants: server_memory hits the live FastAPI server at 127.0.0.1:8766 — fails if server is down
read_logs_sqlite reads directly from iCloud DB path
session_id retries once (0.5s sleep) if the first /session/current response has no session_id — absorbs the race where a brand-new session's first checkpoint write lags behind the query

checkpoint_query reads langgraph_checkpoints.db directly via raw sqlite3 — this IS the live DB SqliteSaver writes to in production (hooks/server.py lifespan()); a prior version of this invariant incorrectly claimed it was deprecated/no-longer-written, never verified against hooks/server.py (corrected 2026-07-05, task:61703ef7)
hooks-mcp-tools-observability.name: hooks-mcp-tools-observability

contracts: setup(name) returns a logger with SQLiteHandler for hook scripts
get_logger(name) returns a 'lc.*' prefixed logger for the LCEL pipeline
logger-sqlite-handler.description: Shared SQLiteHandler that writes all hook and LangChain pipeline logs to claude_hooks.sqlite in iCloud. Singleton per DB path. Redirects to test DB when CLAUDE_HOOKS_TEST_LOG_DB env var is set. Auto-trims at 50k rows.
invariants: SQLiteHandler is a singleton per db_path — never creates duplicate handlers
emit() never raises — handleError() is a no-op
Log table is auto-trimmed to 40k rows when it exceeds 50k
Test redirect affects only the default singleton, not custom-path handlers
logger-sqlite-handler.name: logger-sqlite-handler

contracts: build_dispatcher(mcp) registers all tools from DOMAIN_MAP onto the FastMCP instance
mcp-dispatcher-domain-tool-map.description: FastMCP tool registry. Maps domain names to (module, [action]) pairs. Each action becomes an MCP tool named domain__action. All tool modules live under src/tools/.
invariants: Tool names follow domain__action convention
New tools require adding an entry to DOMAIN_MAP — no auto-discovery
Handler functions must be named handle_<action> in their module
mcp-dispatcher-domain-tool-map.name: mcp-dispatcher-domain-tool-map

contracts: mcp.run() starts the FastMCP server when invoked as __main__
mcp-server-bootstrap.description: MCP server entry point. Calls build_dispatcher to register all tools, then bootstraps by ensuring Ollama is running and rebuilding the task TurboVec embedding index if missing.
invariants: Ollama must be running for semantic task neighbor search to work
Task index is rebuilt at startup only if missing — not on every start
Bootstrap failures are logged and swallowed — never crash the server
mcp-server-bootstrap.name: mcp-server-bootstrap

contracts: Callers of score_memories() can add unbounded domains without any single domain silently excluding another from scoring, as long as no one domain individually exceeds batch_limit.
memory-scoring-per-domain-batch-limit.description: score_memories() fetches candidate memory rows per-domain (not one aggregate query across the whole memories table) before applying combination-signal scoring. Each distinct domain gets its own SELECT ... WHERE domain IS ? ORDER BY updated DESC LIMIT batch_limit, and the per-domain result sets are concatenated before scoring.
invariants: batch_limit (memory_scoring.json / src.config.memory_batch_limit) is a per-domain cap, not an aggregate cap across the whole memories table.
Truncation (when a domain exceeds batch_limit rows) always drops the stalest rows in that domain first, via ORDER BY updated DESC -- never an arbitrary rowid-order slice.
A small or new domain must never be starved out of the candidate pool by a large domain growing past batch_limit -- each domain is queried independently.
memory-scoring-per-domain-batch-limit.name: memory-scoring-per-domain-batch-limit

contracts: normalize_prompt(text) -> str is the single normalization used for both storage key and BM25 tokenization input
lookup_cache(prompt) -> dict|None returns match_type ('exact'|'fuzzy'), source, commit_sha, commits_behind, age_days alongside cache/tags/last_updated

store_cache(prompt, cache, tags='', source='code', domain='', cwd='') upserts on the normalized prompt key (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) — one row per normalized prompt, never duplicates. domain defaults to '' unless given explicitly or inferred from cwd.
delete_cache(prompt) -> {'deleted': bool}, exact-match only (no BM25 fallback for deletes)
list_cache/search_cache (and handle_list/handle_search) accept an optional domain= exact-match filter over the domain column.
prompt-cache-normalize-exact-then-bm25-f.description: Custom homegrown prompt cache (epic c0f3037f) — not Anthropic's cache_control. Stores prompt->answer pairs so recurring 'how does X work?' / design-spec questions, or research answers from WebSearch, can be reused instead of re-derived. Exposed as MCP tools prompt_cache__lookup / __store / __delete. Consumed inside the UPS LangGraph pipeline by CacheCheckNode (langchain_learning/nodes/cache_check.py), wired as load_turn -> cache_check in session_graph.py; result surfaces as SessionState['cache_hit'] and is rendered by dispatcher.py's _format_system_prompt.
invariants: Lookup tries exact match (normalize: lowercase, strip punctuation, collapse whitespace, then equality) first; BM25 (rank-bm25) only runs as a fallback when exact match misses
BM25 fallback hits below _BM25_MIN_SCORE (1.2) are treated as no match, not a weak hit
A cache hit is never served silently — callers must confirm with the user first, and must say so explicitly on a match_type='fuzzy' hit since it's a paraphrase, not the literal question asked

commit_sha/_current_commit_sha/_commits_behind are pinned to _REPO_ROOT (this module's own repo via __file__), never the caller's ambient cwd — an MCP server process inherits whatever cwd it was launched with, which can be a different worktree than the running code
source='websearch' entries always leave commit_sha empty; commits_behind is only meaningful (non-null) for source='code' entries — staleness for websearch entries is tracked via age_days instead
Anti-pollution rule (policy, not code-enforced): only store answers touching 3+ distinct concepts/modules — single-concept or trivial factual answers should not be cached

Cache hit short-circuits the UPS graph: cache_check's conditional edge routes straight to set_prompt_id on a hit, skipping the entire task/memory/tool-hint/RAG fan-out (load_active_task, load_related_tasks, load_task_history, load_task_code, load_related_commits, load_memories, score_tools, cwd_domain_detect) — the cache is a quick-answer lookup, not task context. set_prompt_id always runs regardless (required prompt_id/turn bookkeeping).

domain (task:91dad030) is populated at store time via _domain_from_cwd(cwd) — same cwd_domain_map as src.tools.tasks._domain_from_cwd — or an explicit domain= override. It scopes list_cache/search_cache browsing only. lookup_cache/handle_lookup is deliberately left unscoped by domain — the CacheCheckNode hot path's cross-project reuse (a hit from one project answering a repeat question from another) is validated, documented behavior, not an oversight.
prompt-cache-normalize-exact-then-bm25-f.name: prompt-cache-normalize-exact-then-bm25-fallback

contracts: load_index(tvim_path, meta_path) returns (IdMapIndex | None, dict)
save_index(index, meta, tvim_path, meta_path) is atomic
query_index(index, meta, q_vec, k) returns list[dict] with id, score, plus meta fields
rag-core-turbovec-index.description: Generic TurboVec RAG core shared by code_rag and diff_rag. Provides load/save/query operations on IdMapIndex (.tvim) + .meta.json sidecar pairs. Atomic saves via tmp-then-rename. Callers supply precomputed query vectors.
invariants: Index and metadata sidecar are always written together atomically
load_index returns (None, {}) on any error — callers must handle missing index
query_index is stateless — no caching, no side effects
rag-core-turbovec-index.name: rag-core-turbovec-index

contracts: POST /hook/{event} calls dispatcher handler and returns hookSpecificOutput, a top-level decision/reason dict (Stop only, see dispatcher-hook-event-routing), or {}
GET /health returns {status: ok}
GET /session/memory returns last N events from server_memory
GET /session/current returns {session_id, turn} from the most recent checkpoint — no active task required, unlike GET /session/active
server-fastapi-hook-server.description: Persistent FastAPI server replacing per-invocation subprocess dispatcher. Routes POST /hook/{event} to dispatcher handlers. Uses SqliteSaver for durable LangGraph checkpointing across turns. Trims checkpoint DB to 5 sessions, on every UserPromptSubmit (not just at startup). Forces WAL journal mode on the checkpoint DB connection at startup to eliminate per-write -journal sidecar files.
invariants: Checkpoint DB is trimmed to _CHECKPOINT_SESSION_CAP=5 sessions on EVERY UserPromptSubmit, not just at server startup — a session outside the top-N most-recently-active can be evicted mid-conversation by sibling sessions in other cwds
Checkpoint DB is also trimmed at server startup
Server runs on port 8766

Checkpoint DB connection runs in WAL journal mode, forced via PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL at lifespan startup (before SqliteSaver is opened) — the file previously ran in SQLite's default rollback-journal ('delete') mode, where every write creates+deletes a `-journal` sidecar; a transient failure doing that surfaced as 'attempt to write a readonly database' independent of restart timing (task:ac5df3db, fixed 2026-07-08)
server-fastapi-hook-server.name: server-fastapi-hook-server

contracts: record_prompt/record_tool/record_task write to both layers
get(n_events) returns from in-memory cache only (no DB hit at read time)
server-memory-consolidated-timeline.description: Two-layer consolidated context store (SQLite + in-memory cache) answering 'what was I working on?'. Stores prompts, MCP tool calls, and task activations as a chronological event sequence. Write-through: every insert hits both SQLite and the in-memory cache.
invariants: Test sessions (prefixed test-, pytest-, api-test-) are never written to the store
Rolling window capped at 1000 events — oldest deleted on overflow
Cache is hydrated from SQLite at server startup so context survives reloads
server-memory-consolidated-timeline.name: server-memory-consolidated-timeline

contracts: record_tool_from_hook(body) and record_task_from_hook(body) take raw PostToolUse dicts
server-memory-tool-recording.description: Hook payload helpers that extract and record tool calls from raw PostToolUse payloads. MCP tools recorded with compact JSON args. Read/Edit/Write recorded with file_path. Bash only recorded for version-control operations. tasks__set_active recorded as task type not tool type.
invariants: tasks__set_active is recorded as 'task' event, not 'tool' event
Bash non-VCS calls are silently skipped
Args truncated to 300 chars to cap storage
server-memory-tool-recording.name: server-memory-tool-recording

contracts: LoadTaskCodeNode.__call__ resolves .code_embeddings.tvim/.meta.json from Path(state['cwd']) first
LoadRelatedCommitsNode._resolve_diff_index(cwd) resolves .diff_embeddings.tvim/.meta.json from Path(cwd) first, returns (index, meta)
Both fall back to a hardcoded claude-hooks default path only when no index exists at the cwd-derived path
ups-nodes-cwd-first-repo-resolution.description: Repeated pattern across UPS task-context fan-out nodes: any node resolving a repo-scoped index/resource must try state['cwd']'s repo first, falling back to a hardcoded default repo (claude-hooks) only if nothing exists at cwd. LoadTaskCodeNode (langchain_learning/nodes/load_task_code.py) established this; LoadRelatedCommitsNode (langchain_learning/nodes/load_related_commits.py) was fixed to match it in task:4b7ccfe8 after being found to always query claude-hooks' diff index regardless of the active task's actual repo.
invariants: A task can be activated from any repo, not just claude-hooks — a node that skips the cwd check silently returns data from the wrong repo instead of failing loudly or returning empty
cwd-derived resolution is tried first and only falls back to the default repo path if the cwd-scoped resource is missing, never the other way around
New UPS nodes that read a repo-local file/index (TurboVec .tvim, git log, etc.) must follow this same resolution order
ups-nodes-cwd-first-repo-resolution.name: ups-nodes-cwd-first-repo-resolution
